preamble: The transformation from a palatalized voiced velar stop to an alveolar flap is a rare but documented example of extreme lenition and fronting, most notably seen in the evolution of Proto-Austronesian reflexes.

examples.note: Reconstructed shift where the palatalized velar stop weakens into a rhotic liquid in various daughter languages.

sources: Wolff, J. U. (1988). The PAN Consonant System.
